analysis: Seven nights in a row above six hours, with four of them clearing seven and a half — that's not a fluke, that's a body that trusts the routine you've built around it. Your best night was 8 July at nearly eight hours with only 32 minutes awake, and your REM has been genuinely impressive all week, hitting two hours on both the 6th and 8th and staying above an hour and a half every single night. The one thing worth noticing is that your awake time spiked on the 7th and again on the 10th — over an hour both nights — which suggests something's occasionally pulling you out of sleep mid-cycle even when the total looks decent. Deep sleep has been a little lean most nights, mostly in the 26–53 minute range, so if you haven't already, that cold blast after the hot shower is your friend for squeezing more of that out.
